name: openEuler sig-ruby SIG
description: Developed by Yukihiro "Matz" Matsumoto, ruby combines his favorite languages (Perl, Smalltalk, Eiffel, ADA and LISP) to create a new language with both functional and imperative programming features.

acceptedAnswer.text: openEuler sig-ruby SIG (Special Interest Group) is responsible for Developed by Yukihiro "Matz" Matsumoto, ruby combines his favorite languages (Perl, Smalltalk, Eiffel, ADA and LISP) to create a new language with both functional and imperative programming features.. The SIG has 12 core members and maintains 273 repositories in the Infrastructure and Tools domain.

mainEntity.name: How to participate in openEuler sig-ruby SIG?

acceptedAnswer.text: To participate in openEuler sig-ruby SIG: 1) Submit Pull Requests to relevant repositories; 2) Subscribe to the mailing list at Ruby@openeuler.org; 3) Attend SIG regular meetings, see the openEuler website calendar for details.

mainEntity.name: What repositories does openEuler sig-ruby SIG include?

acceptedAnswer.text: openEuler sig-ruby SIG maintains 273 repositories. Full list at https://www.openeuler.org/en/sig/sig-ruby.

mainEntity.name: Who are the Maintainers of openEuler sig-ruby SIG?

acceptedAnswer.text: openEuler sig-ruby SIG has 2 Maintainers, including: xinwei Hu, senlin Xia. Maintainers are responsible for final code review and merging.
